text:
TZra meteor produces only a quarter of the effects; the
Dhisnya, a half of it; while the remaining three produce their effects
in full measure.
The thunderbolt falls with a deafening sound upon men,
elephants, horses, beasts, rocks, houses, trees and domestic animals,
revolving like a discus and piercing the surface of the earth.
[Cf. the etrmsfim—.

Lightning, which is crooked and extensive, falls suddenly on
living beings and heaped up fire-wood, setting them ablaze, causing
fright to the animals and producing a rattling sound.
Cf. the tftmmf^TT(?) —
it ^cft m mhi^H \\\\\
The DhisnyU is a slender thing with a short tail with a body
two cubits long. It resembles burning embers and appears quite
distinct over an area of forty cubits.
[Cf. the abridged work :—
